text: Hand osteoarthritis with aching knuckles, worsening over time; pain 3/10 at rest, up to 5/10 by end of shift
transcript_quote: You told me a while back it was osteoarthritis, and it has been creeping... Right now, a three. End of a Friday shift, a five maybe.

text: Morning finger stiffness that improves with warmth/coffee; aggravated by 10 hours of gripping totes and scanning at distribution center job
transcript_quote: by end of shift my fingers feel like rusty hinges. Mornings they are stiff until the coffee kicks in.

text: Denies joint swelling, heat, redness, locking, or dropping objects in hands

slots.negatives: no swelling, heat, redness, locking, dropping

transcript_quote: No dropping, no hot or red. Just that deep ache.

text: Uses OTC naproxen 220 mg with food, one to two times per week for hand pain
transcript_quote: The naproxen helps — the over-the-counter 220s. One, maybe two a week, with food, like you said last time.

text: Denies GI symptoms with NSAID use — no stomach burning, no black stools
transcript_quote: No, none of that. I am careful.

text: Advised to continue naproxen with food, occasional use as currently doing
transcript_quote: keep taking it with food, keep it occasional exactly like you are doing

text: Counseled on hand exercises (tendon glides) and morning warm soaks for arthritis
transcript_quote: I will show you some hand exercises — tendon glides, and a warm soak in the morning to loosen the hinges

text: Reports financial stress and anxiety with racing thoughts, midnight rumination about finances after spouse's hours were cut
transcript_quote: Money, mostly. Shauna's hours got cut in the spring... I lie there at midnight running numbers.

text: Reports daytime tension, shoulder tightness, and irritability/snapping at coworkers
transcript_quote: I am tense all day — shoulders up around my ears, snapping at guys at work over nothing.

text: Reports episodic chest tightness (band-like) associated with anxiety, not with exertion; stairs and physical work cause no symptoms
transcript_quote: sometimes my chest gets tight. Not pain. Tight, like a band... When the worry spins up. Stairs I do all shift, no problem.

text: GAD-7 score of 12, moderate anxiety range
transcript_quote: the GAD-7 — you scored a 12, which lands in the moderate range

text: PHQ-2 positive screen with score of 5
transcript_quote: The short screener flagged you — a 5

text: PHQ-9 score of 4, minimal depression; retains interest, normal appetite, adequate energy, no hopelessness
transcript_quote: on the full version you come out at a 4, which is minimal

text: Reports thin/reduced sleep due to nighttime worry
transcript_quote: Sleep is thin because of the number-spinning.

text: Denies suicidal ideation or self-harm thoughts
transcript_quote: No. God, no. I have too much to stick around for.

text: Assessment: anxiety (stuck alarm), not depression or cardiac cause
transcript_quote: that sounds like anxiety, not your heart

text: Counseled on regular aerobic exercise: brisk 20-30 minute walk after dinner most days to reduce anxiety
transcript_quote: A brisk walk after dinner, twenty or thirty minutes, most days

text: Counseled on 'scheduled worry' technique — 15 minutes same time each evening for budgeting
transcript_quote: schedule the worry. Fifteen minutes, same time every evening, pen and paper, do all the budgeting then.

text: Referred to counseling center on Fairview with sliding-scale fees for anxiety therapy
transcript_quote: there is a counseling center over on Fairview that runs a sliding scale

text: No psychiatric medication started today; option deferred, to reconsider if worry worsens
transcript_quote: And no medicines for this today either — that is not where we are

text: Return precautions: come back sooner if worry disrupts sleep nightly or chest tightness worsens
transcript_quote: if the worry starts eating your sleep every night, or that chest tightness ramps up, you come back sooner

text: AUDIT-C score of 2; reports about two beers on Saturdays
transcript_quote: Two beers, tops.

text: Never smoked tobacco
transcript_quote: Never smoked, right?

text: Blood pressure 102/78 mmHg
transcript_quote: Blood pressure is 102 over 78 — excellent.

text: Heart rate 74/min
transcript_quote: Pulse 74

text: Respiratory rate 15/min
transcript_quote: breathing 15

text: Height 162.1 cm, weight 73.5 kg, BMI 27.96 (down from prior)
transcript_quote: You are 162.1 centimeters and 73.5 kilos, which works out to a BMI just under 28 — you are actually down

text: Heart and lungs auscultated normal
transcript_quote: Heart and lungs sound great.

text: Hand exam: bony fullness at several knuckles, mild tenderness to palpation, no swelling, no heat, strong grip — consistent with osteoarthritis
transcript_quote: I can feel that bony fullness at a few of the knuckles, little tender when I press, but no swelling, no heat, and your grip is strong.

text: Dental problem: lower left posterior tooth with cold sensitivity/twinges; no dental care since child's birth
transcript_quote: Lower left, in the back. Twinges on cold drinks. I have not sat in a dentist's chair since Ellie was born

text: Referred for dental care to sliding-scale clinic
transcript_quote: I am putting in a dental referral — and the clinic I refer to runs sliding scale too

text: Lipid panel: total cholesterol 157.54 mg/dL, LDL 101.02 mg/dL, triglycerides 134.84 mg/dL
transcript_quote: total 157.54, LDL 101.02, triglycerides 134.84

text: Low HDL cholesterol 29.55 mg/dL; counseled that exercise raises HDL
transcript_quote: The one soft spot is the HDL, the protective one — 29.55, and we like that higher.

text: Influenza vaccine administered
transcript_quote: The flu vaccine.

text: Td (tetanus) booster administered — patient overdue, works in warehouse
transcript_quote: Your tetanus booster — you are overdue

text: Hepatitis A vaccine (adult) administered
transcript_quote: And hepatitis A, which your generation mostly never got as kids.

text: Follow-up in two to three months to reassess anxiety trend
transcript_quote: Come back in two or three months regardless, just to tell me which way the worry is trending.

text: SDOH: financial strain due to spouse's reduced work hours, single income supporting household of 3 including second-grade child; annual family income ~$46,526
transcript_quote: Shauna's hours got cut in the spring. We have Ellie in second grade, so we are one income and change now

text: SDOH: has stable housing, feels safe at home, full-time employment, private insurance, high school/GED education, no transportation barriers
Occupation: picker/warehouse worker, 10-hour shifts of gripping and walking on concrete
transcript_quote: I am a picker at the distribution center — ten hours of gripping totes and scanning

text: Medication reconciliation completed
